methodology_pattern_or_risk: Scholars may explain different hadith wordings or routes while appearing to discuss the same broad proof.
why_it_matters: Blind separation can make distinct explanation contexts look interchangeable.
risk_if_over-ruled_by_hard_rules: The library pairs explanations with the wrong proof instance.
required_safeguard_direction: Version-aware linkage plus uncertainty gates when exact alignment is unclear.

methodology_pattern_or_risk: Scholars may presuppose different grading/authenticity assumptions while explaining the same hadith family.
why_it_matters: A fetched canonical proof alone may not reflect the assumptions the explanation was built on.
risk_if_over-ruled_by_hard_rules: False sense that all explanations map to one uniform source truth in the same way.
required_safeguard_direction: Preserve grading-sensitive context where relevant and do not force flattening.

methodology_pattern_or_risk: Some scholars interweave ruling, proof, and explanation too tightly for clean prewritten boundaries.
why_it_matters: A rigid separation protocol can corrupt the reasoning flow instead of clarifying it.
required_safeguard_direction: Case-level intelligent diagnosis instead of blind rules alone.

methodology_pattern_or_risk: Unseen future methodologies or source patterns may appear that current rules do not cover.
why_it_matters: The library cannot assume all edge cases are already known.
risk_if_over-ruled_by_hard_rules: Silent failure on novel patterns and false confidence in automation completeness.
required_safeguard_direction: Uncertainty gates, escalation paths, and a bias toward caution under uncertainty.

methodology_pattern_or_risk: Generated notes may create readability while hiding unresolved integrity problems underneath.
why_it_matters: A readable orphaned explanation can still be structurally wrong.
risk_if_over-ruled_by_hard_rules: The system mistakes surface comprehensibility for genuine source integrity.
required_safeguard_direction: Treat notes as secondary aids, not proof that the underlying split was valid.
